  .schema('myschema')
  .from('mytable')
  .select()

definitions.name: update
definitions.description:
Perform an UPDATE on the table or view.

By default, updated rows are not returned. To return it, chain the call
with `.select()` after filters.
remarks: - `update()` should always be combined with [Filters](/docs/reference/javascript/using-filters) to target the item(s) you wish to update.

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: The values to update with

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Named parameters

properties.name: count
properties.description:
Count algorithm to use to count updated rows.

`"exact"`: Exact but slow count algorithm. Performs a `COUNT(*)` under the
hood.

`"planned"`: Approximated but fast count algorithm. Uses the Postgres
statistics under the hood.

`"estimated"`: Uses exact count for low numbers and planned count for high
numbers.

properties.name: containedBy
properties.description:
Only relevant for jsonb, array, and range columns. Match only rows where
every element appearing in `column` is contained by `value`.

properties.name: contains
properties.description:
Only relevant for jsonb, array, and range columns. Match only rows where
`column` contains every element appearing in `value`.

properties.name: filter
properties.description:
Match only rows which satisfy the filter. This is an escape hatch - you
should use the specific filter methods wherever possible.

Unlike most filters, `opearator` and `value` are used as-is and need to
follow [PostgREST
syntax](https://postgrest.org/en/stable/api.html#operators). You also need
to make sure they are properly sanitized.

properties.name: gt
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` is greater than `value`.

properties.name: gte
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` is greater than or equal to `value`.

properties.name: ilike
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches `pattern` case-insensitively.

properties.name: ilikeAllOf
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches all of `patterns` case-insensitively.

properties.name: ilikeAnyOf
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches any of `patterns` case-insensitively.

properties.name: is
properties.description:
Match only rows where `column` IS `value`.

For non-boolean columns, this is only relevant for checking if the value of
`column` is NULL by setting `value` to `null`.

For boolean columns, you can also set `value` to `true` or `false` and it
will behave the same way as `.eq()`.

properties.name: like
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches `pattern` case-sensitively.

properties.name: likeAllOf
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches all of `patterns` case-sensitively.

properties.name: likeAnyOf
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches any of `patterns` case-sensitively.

properties.name: lt
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` is less than `value`.

properties.name: lte
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` is less than or equal to `value`.

properties.name: match
properties.description:
Match only rows where each column in `query` keys is equal to its
associated value. Shorthand for multiple `.eq()`s.

properties.name: not
properties.description:
Match only rows which doesn't satisfy the filter.

Unlike most filters, `opearator` and `value` are used as-is and need to
follow [PostgREST
syntax](https://postgrest.org/en/stable/api.html#operators). You also need
to make sure they are properly sanitized.

properties.name: order
properties.description:
Order the query result by `column`.

You can call this method multiple times to order by multiple columns.

You can order referenced tables, but it only affects the ordering of the
parent table if you use `!inner` in the query.

properties.name: overlaps
properties.description:
Only relevant for array and range columns. Match only rows where
`column` and `value` have an element in common.

properties.name: rangeAdjacent
properties.description:
Only relevant for range columns. Match only rows where `column` is
mutually exclusive to `range` and there can be no element between the two
ranges.

properties.name: rangeGt
properties.description:
Only relevant for range columns. Match only rows where every element in
`column` is greater than any element in `range`.

properties.name: rangeGte
properties.description:
Only relevant for range columns. Match only rows where every element in
`column` is either contained in `range` or greater than any element in
`range`.

properties.name: rangeLt
properties.description:
Only relevant for range columns. Match only rows where every element in
`column` is less than any element in `range`.

properties.name: rangeLte
properties.description:
Only relevant for range columns. Match only rows where every element in
`column` is either contained in `range` or less than any element in
`range`.

properties.name: regexIMatch
properties.description:
Match only rows where `column` matches the PostgreSQL regex `pattern`
case-insensitively (using the `~*` operator).

properties.name: regexMatch
properties.description:
Match only rows where `column` matches the PostgreSQL regex `pattern`
case-sensitively (using the `~` operator).

properties.name: textSearch
properties.description:
Only relevant for text and tsvector columns. Match only rows where
`column` matches the query string in `query`.

examples.title: Updating your data
examples.code:
const { error } = await supabase
  .from('instruments')
  .update({ name: 'piano' })
  .eq('id', 1)
examples.notes:
Updating JSON data
Postgres offers some
[operators](/docs/guides/database/json#query-the-jsonb-data) for
working with JSON data. Currently, it is only possible to update the entire JSON document.

examples.title: Update a record and return it
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('instruments')
  .update({ name: 'piano' })
  .eq('id', 1)
  .select()

definitions.name: upsert
definitions.description:
Perform an UPSERT on the table or view. Depending on the column(s) passed
to `onConflict`, `.upsert()` allows you to perform the equivalent of
`.insert()` if a row with the corresponding `onConflict` columns doesn't
exist, or if it does exist, perform an alternative action depending on
`ignoreDuplicates`.

By default, upserted rows are not returned. To return it, chain the call
with `.select()`.
remarks: - Primary keys must be included in `values` to use upsert.

examples.title: Upsert a single row using a unique key
examples.notes:
Upserting into tables with constraints
In the following query,  implicitly uses the 
(primary key) column to determine conflicts. If there is no existing
row with the same ,  inserts a new row, which
will fail in this case as there is already a row with .
Using the  option, you can instruct  to use
another column with a unique constraint to determine conflicts.

examples.title: Upsert with conflict resolution and exact row counting
Upsert your data
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('instruments')
  .upsert({ id: 1, name: 'piano' })
  .select()

examples.title: Bulk Upsert your data
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('instruments')
  .upsert([
    { id: 1, name: 'piano' },
    { id: 2, name: 'harp' },
  ])
  .select()

examples.title: Upserting into tables with constraints
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('users')
  .upsert({ id: 42, handle: 'saoirse', display_name: 'Saoirse' })
  .select()

definitions.description: Creates a builder configured for a specific PostgREST request.

properties.name: order

Creating a Postgrest query builder

definitions.name: abortSignal
definitions.description: Set the AbortSignal for the fetch request.
remarks: You can use this to set a timeout for the request.

parameters.name: signal
parameters.description: The AbortSignal to use for the fetch request

examples.title: Aborting requests in-flight
examples.code:
const ac = new AbortController()

const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('very_big_table')
  .select()
  .abortSignal(ac.signal)

// Abort the request after 100 ms
setTimeout(() => ac.abort(), 100)
examples.notes:
](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/AbortController) to abort requests.
Note that  and  don't mean anything for aborted requests as the request wasn't fulfilled.

examples.title: Set a timeout
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('very_big_table')
  .select()
  .abortSignal(AbortSignal.timeout(1000 /* ms */))

definitions.name: csv
definitions.description: Return `data` as a string in CSV format.

examples.title: Return data as CSV
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('characters')
  .select()
  .csv()
examples.sql:
create table
  characters (id int8 primary key, name text);

insert into
  characters (id, name)
values
  (1, 'Luke'),
  (2, 'Leia'),
  (3, 'Han');

definitions.name: explain
definitions.description:
Return `data` as the EXPLAIN plan for the query.

You need to enable the
[db_plan_enabled](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/debugging-performance#enabling-explain)
setting before using this method.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Named parameters

properties.name: analyze
properties.description:
If `true`, the query will be executed and the
actual run time will be returned

properties.name: buffers
properties.description: If `true`, include information on buffer usage

properties.name: format
properties.description:
The format of the output, can be `"text"` (default)
or `"json"`

properties.name: settings
properties.description:
If `true`, include information on configuration
parameters that affect query planning

properties.name: verbose
properties.description:
If `true`, the query identifier will be returned
and `data` will include the output columns of the query

properties.name: wal
properties.description: If `true`, include information on WAL record generation

examples.title: Get the execution plan
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('characters')
  .select()
  .explain()

insert into
  characters (id, name)
values
  (1, 'Luke'),

definitions.name: limit

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: The maximum number of rows to return

parameters.name: options

properties.name: foreignTable

Deprecated, use `options.referencedTable`
instead

Set this to limit rows of referenced
tables instead of the parent table

examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('characters')
  .select('name')
  .limit(1)
examples.sql: `select()`

examples.title: On a referenced table
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('orchestral_sections')
  .select(`
    name,
    instruments (
      name
    )
  `)
  .limit(1, { referencedTable: 'instruments' })
examples.sql:
create table
  orchestral_sections (id int8 primary key, name text);
create table
  instruments (
    id int8 primary key,
    section_id int8 not null references orchestral_sections,
    name text
  );

insert into
  orchestral_sections (id, name)
values
  (1, 'strings');
insert into
  instruments (id, section_id, name)
values
  (1, 1, 'harp'),
  (2, 1, 'violin');

definitions.name: maxAffected
definitions.description:
Set the maximum number of rows that can be affected by the query.
Only available in PostgREST v13+ and only works with PATCH and DELETE methods.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The maximum number of rows that can be affected

definitions.name: maybeSingle
definitions.description:
Return `data` as a single object instead of an array of objects.

Query result must be zero or one row (e.g. using `.limit(1)`), otherwise
this returns an error.

examples.title: With `select()`
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('characters')
  .select()
  .eq('name', 'Katniss')
  .maybeSingle()

You can call this method multiple times to order by multiple columns.

You can order referenced tables, but it only affects the ordering of the
parent table if you use `!inner` in the query.

examples.title: With `select()`
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('characters')
  .select('id, name')
  .order('id', { ascending: false })

On a referenced table
Ordering with  doesn't affect the ordering of the

const { data, error } = await supabase
    .from('orchestral_sections')
    .select(`
      name,
      instruments (
        name
      )
    `)
    .order('name', { referencedTable: 'instruments', ascending: false })
examples.sql:
create table
  orchestral_sections (id int8 primary key, name text);
create table
  instruments (
    id int8 primary key,
    section_id int8 not null references orchestral_sections,
    name text
  );

insert into
  orchestral_sections (id, name)
values
  (1, 'strings'),
  (2, 'woodwinds');
insert into
  instruments (id, section_id, name)
values
  (1, 1, 'harp'),
  (2, 1, 'violin');
examples.notes:
doesn't affect the ordering of the
parent table.

examples.title: Order parent table by a referenced table
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
    .from('instruments')
    .select(`
      name,
      section:orchestral_sections (
        name
      )
    `)
    .order('section(name)', { ascending: true })
examples.sql:
create table
  orchestral_sections (id int8 primary key, name text);
create table
  instruments (
    id int8 primary key,
    section_id int8 not null references orchestral_sections,
    name text
  );

insert into
  orchestral_sections (id, name)
values
  (1, 'strings'),
  (2, 'woodwinds');
insert into
  instruments (id, section_id, name)
values
  (1, 2, 'flute'),
  (2, 1, 'violin');
examples.notes:
affects the ordering of the
parent table.

definitions.name: overrideTypes
definitions.description: Override the type of the returned `data` field in the response.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.code:
// Merge with existing types (default behavior)
const query = supabase
  .from('users')
  .select()
  .overrideTypes<{ custom_field: string }>()

// Replace existing types completely
const replaceQuery = supabase
  .from('users')
  .select()
  .overrideTypes<{ id: number; name: string }, { merge: false }>()

examples.title: Complete Override type of successful response
examples.code:
const { data } = await supabase
  .from('countries')
  .select()
  .overrideTypes<Array<MyType>, { merge: false }>()

examples.title: Complete Override type of object response
examples.code:
const { data } = await supabase
  .from('countries')
  .select()
  .maybeSingle()
  .overrideTypes<MyType, { merge: false }>()
examples.response: let x: typeof data // MyType | null

examples.title: Partial Override type of successful response
examples.code:
const { data } = await supabase
  .from('countries')
  .select()
  .overrideTypes<Array<{ status: "A" | "B" }>>()
examples.response: let x: typeof data // Array<CountryRowProperties & { status: "A" | "B" }>

examples.title: Partial Override type of object response
examples.code:
const { data } = await supabase
  .from('countries')
  .select()
  .maybeSingle()
  .overrideTypes<{ status: "A" | "B" }>()
examples.response: let x: typeof data // CountryRowProperties & { status: "A" | "B" } | null

examples.title: Example 5
examples.code:
// Merge with existing types (default behavior)
const query = supabase
  .from('users')
  .select()
  .overrideTypes<{ custom_field: string }>()

// Replace existing types completely
const replaceQuery = supabase
  .from('users')
  .select()
  .overrideTypes<{ id: number; name: string }, { merge: false }>()

definitions.name: range
definitions.description:
Limit the query result by starting at an offset `from` and ending at the offset `to`.
Only records within this range are returned.
This respects the query order and if there is no order clause the range could behave unexpectedly.
The `from` and `to` values are 0-based and inclusive: `range(1, 3)` will include the second, third
and fourth rows of the query.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The starting index from which to limit the result

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The last index to which to limit the result

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Named parameters

properties.name: foreignTable
properties.description:
Deprecated, use `options.referencedTable`
instead

properties.name: referencedTable
properties.description:
Set this to limit rows of referenced
tables instead of the parent table

examples.title: With `select()`
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('characters')
  .select('name')
  .range(0, 1)
examples.sql: `select()`

definitions.name: returns
definitions.description: Override the type of the returned `data`.
remarks: - Deprecated: use overrideTypes method instead

properties.name: order
properties.description:
Order the query result by `column`.

You can call this method multiple times to order by multiple columns.

You can order referenced tables, but it only affects the ordering of the
parent table if you use `!inner` in the query.

examples.title: Override type of successful response
examples.code:
const { data } = await supabase
  .from('countries')
  .select()
  .returns<Array<MyType>>()

examples.title: Override type of object response
examples.code:
const { data } = await supabase
  .from('countries')
  .select()
  .maybeSingle()
  .returns<MyType>()
examples.response: let x: typeof data // MyType | null

definitions.name: rollback
definitions.description:
Rollback the query.

`data` will still be returned, but the query is not committed.

definitions.name: select
definitions.description:
Perform a SELECT on the query result.

By default, `.insert()`, `.update()`, `.upsert()`, and `.delete()` do not
return modified rows. By calling this method, modified rows are returned in
`data`.

parameters.name: columns
parameters.description: The columns to retrieve, separated by commas

properties.name: containedBy
properties.description:
Only relevant for jsonb, array, and range columns. Match only rows where
every element appearing in `column` is contained by `value`.

properties.name: contains
properties.description:
Only relevant for jsonb, array, and range columns. Match only rows where
`column` contains every element appearing in `value`.

properties.name: filter
properties.description:
Match only rows which satisfy the filter. This is an escape hatch - you
should use the specific filter methods wherever possible.

Unlike most filters, `opearator` and `value` are used as-is and need to
follow [PostgREST
syntax](https://postgrest.org/en/stable/api.html#operators). You also need
to make sure they are properly sanitized.

properties.name: gt
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` is greater than `value`.

properties.name: gte
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` is greater than or equal to `value`.

properties.name: ilike
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches `pattern` case-insensitively.

properties.name: ilikeAllOf
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches all of `patterns` case-insensitively.

properties.name: ilikeAnyOf
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches any of `patterns` case-insensitively.

properties.name: is
properties.description:
Match only rows where `column` IS `value`.

For non-boolean columns, this is only relevant for checking if the value of
`column` is NULL by setting `value` to `null`.

For boolean columns, you can also set `value` to `true` or `false` and it
will behave the same way as `.eq()`.

properties.name: like
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches `pattern` case-sensitively.

properties.name: likeAllOf
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches all of `patterns` case-sensitively.

properties.name: likeAnyOf
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` matches any of `patterns` case-sensitively.

properties.name: lt
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` is less than `value`.

properties.name: lte
properties.description: Match only rows where `column` is less than or equal to `value`.

properties.name: match
properties.description:
Match only rows where each column in `query` keys is equal to its
associated value. Shorthand for multiple `.eq()`s.

properties.name: not
properties.description:
Match only rows which doesn't satisfy the filter.

Unlike most filters, `opearator` and `value` are used as-is and need to
follow [PostgREST
syntax](https://postgrest.org/en/stable/api.html#operators). You also need
to make sure they are properly sanitized.

properties.name: order
properties.description:
Order the query result by `column`.

You can call this method multiple times to order by multiple columns.

You can order referenced tables, but it only affects the ordering of the
parent table if you use `!inner` in the query.

properties.name: overlaps
properties.description:
Only relevant for array and range columns. Match only rows where
`column` and `value` have an element in common.

properties.name: rangeAdjacent
properties.description:
Only relevant for range columns. Match only rows where `column` is
mutually exclusive to `range` and there can be no element between the two
ranges.

properties.name: rangeGt
properties.description:
Only relevant for range columns. Match only rows where every element in
`column` is greater than any element in `range`.

properties.name: rangeGte
properties.description:
Only relevant for range columns. Match only rows where every element in
`column` is either contained in `range` or greater than any element in
`range`.

properties.name: rangeLt
properties.description:
Only relevant for range columns. Match only rows where every element in
`column` is less than any element in `range`.

properties.name: rangeLte
properties.description:
Only relevant for range columns. Match only rows where every element in
`column` is either contained in `range` or less than any element in
`range`.

properties.name: regexIMatch
properties.description:
Match only rows where `column` matches the PostgreSQL regex `pattern`
case-insensitively (using the `~*` operator).

properties.name: regexMatch
properties.description:
Match only rows where `column` matches the PostgreSQL regex `pattern`
case-sensitively (using the `~` operator).

properties.name: textSearch
properties.description:
Only relevant for text and tsvector columns. Match only rows where
`column` matches the query string in `query`.

examples.title: With `upsert()`
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('characters')
  .upsert({ id: 1, name: 'Han Solo' })
  .select()
examples.sql: `upsert()`

definitions.name: setHeader
definitions.description: Set an HTTP header for the request.

definitions.name: single
definitions.description:
Return `data` as a single object instead of an array of objects.

Query result must be one row (e.g. using `.limit(1)`), otherwise this
returns an error.

examples.title: With `select()`
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('characters')
  .select('name')
  .limit(1)
  .single()

definitions.name: throwOnError
definitions.description:
If there's an error with the query, throwOnError will reject the promise by
throwing the error instead of returning it as part of a successful response.

https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/issues/92

You can order referenced tables, but it only affects the ordering of the
parent table if you use `!inner` in the query.

definitions.name: constructor
definitions.description:
Creates a channel that can broadcast messages, sync presence, and listen to Postgres changes.

The topic determines which realtime stream you are subscribing to. Config options let you
enable acknowledgement for broadcasts, presence tracking, or private channels.

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: Topic name can be any string.

properties.name: broadcast
properties.description:
self option enables client to receive message it broadcast
ack option instructs server to acknowledge that broadcast message was received
replay option instructs server to replay broadcast messages

properties.name: presence
properties.description: key option is used to track presence payload across clients

properties.name: private
properties.description: defines if the channel is private or not and if RLS policies will be used to check data

definitions.name: httpSend
definitions.description:
Sends a broadcast message explicitly via REST API.

This method always uses the REST API endpoint regardless of WebSocket connection state.
Useful when you want to guarantee REST delivery or when gradually migrating from implicit REST fallback.

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: The name of the broadcast event

parameters.name: payload
parameters.description: Payload to be sent (required)

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options including timeout

definitions.name: on
definitions.description: Listen to realtime events on this channel.
remarks:
- By default, Broadcast and Presence are enabled for all projects.
- By default, listening to database changes is disabled for new projects due to database performance and security concerns. You can turn it on by managing Realtime's [replication](/docs/guides/api#realtime-api-overview).
- You can receive the "previous" data for updates and deletes by setting the table's `REPLICA IDENTITY` to `FULL` (e.g., `ALTER TABLE your_table REPLICA IDENTITY FULL;`).
- Row level security is not applied to delete statements. When RLS is enabled and replica identity is set to full, only the primary key is sent to clients.

properties.name: broadcast
properties.description:
self option enables client to receive message it broadcast
ack option instructs server to acknowledge that broadcast message was received
replay option instructs server to replay broadcast messages

properties.name: presence
properties.description: key option is used to track presence payload across clients

properties.name: private
properties.description: defines if the channel is private or not and if RLS policies will be used to check data

properties.name: channel
properties.description: The realtime channel to bind to.

properties.name: topic
properties.description: Topic name can be any string.

properties.name: on
properties.description: Listen to realtime events on this channel.

examples.title: Listen to broadcast messages
examples.notes:
Listen to updates
By default, Supabase will send only the updated record. If you want to receive the previous values as well you can
enable full replication for the table you are listening to:

examples.title: Listen to presence sync
examples.notes:
Listen to deletes
By default, Supabase does not send deleted records. If you want to receive the deleted record you can
enable full replication for the table you are listening to:

examples.title: Listen to presence join
examples.notes:
Listen to multiple events
You can chain listeners if you want to listen to multiple events for each table.

examples.title: Listen to presence leave
examples.notes:
Listen to row level changes
You can listen to individual rows using the format  - where  is the column name, and  is the value which you want to match.

examples.title: Listen to all database changes
Listen to a specific table
Listen to inserts
Listen to updates
Listen to deletes
Listen to multiple events
examples.code:
supabase
  .channel('room1')
  .on('postgres_changes', { event: 'INSERT', schema: 'public', table: 'countries' }, handleRecordInserted)
  .on('postgres_changes', { event: 'DELETE', schema: 'public', table: 'countries' }, handleRecordDeleted)
  .subscribe()

examples.title: Listen to row level changes
examples.code:
supabase
  .channel('room1')
  .on('postgres_changes', { event: 'UPDATE', schema: 'public', table: 'countries', filter: 'id=eq.200' }, handleRecordUpdated)
  .subscribe()
examples.notes: - where  is the column name, and  is the value which you want to match.

definitions.name: presenceState
definitions.description:
Returns the current presence state for this channel.

The shape is a map keyed by presence key (for example a user id) where each entry contains the
tracked metadata for that user.

definitions.name: send
definitions.description: Sends a message into the channel.
remarks:
- When using REST you don't need to subscribe to the channel
- REST calls are only available from 2.37.0 onwards

parameters.name: opts

examples.title: Send a message via websocket

supabase
  .channel('room1')
  .httpSend('cursor-pos', { x: Math.random(), y: Math.random() })

definitions.name: teardown
definitions.description: Destroys and stops related timers.

definitions.name: track
definitions.description:
Sends the supplied payload to the presence tracker so other subscribers can see that this
client is online. Use `untrack` to stop broadcasting presence for the same key.

definitions.name: unsubscribe
definitions.description:
Leaves the channel.

Unsubscribes from server events, and instructs channel to terminate on server.
Triggers onClose() hooks.

To receive leave acknowledgements, use the a `receive` hook to bind to the server ack, ie:
channel.unsubscribe().receive("ok", () => alert("left!") )

definitions.name: untrack
definitions.description: Removes the current presence state for this client.

definitions.name: updateJoinPayload
definitions.description:
Updates the payload that will be sent the next time the channel joins (reconnects).
Useful for rotating access tokens or updating config without re-creating the channel.

Push out a message if the socket is connected.

If the socket is not connected, the message gets enqueued within a local buffer, and sent out when a connection is next established.

definitions.name: setAuth
definitions.description:
Sets the JWT access token used for channel subscription authorization and Realtime RLS.

If param is null it will use the `accessToken` callback function or the token set on the client.

On callback used, it will set the value of the token internal to the client.

When a token is explicitly provided, it will be preserved across channel operations
(including removeChannel and resubscribe). The `accessToken` callback will not be
invoked until `setAuth()` is called without arguments.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: A JWT string to override the token set on the client.

definitions.name: httpSend
definitions.description:
Sends a broadcast message explicitly via REST API.

This method always uses the REST API endpoint regardless of WebSocket connection state.
Useful when you want to guarantee REST delivery or when gradually migrating from implicit REST fallback.

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: The name of the broadcast event

parameters.name: payload
parameters.description: Payload to be sent (required)

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options including timeout

definitions.name: presenceState
definitions.description:
Returns the current presence state for this channel.

The shape is a map keyed by presence key (for example a user id) where each entry contains the
tracked metadata for that user.

definitions.name: send
definitions.description: Sends a message into the channel.
remarks:
- When using REST you don't need to subscribe to the channel
- REST calls are only available from 2.37.0 onwards

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Arguments to send to channel

properties.name: event
properties.description: The name of the event being sent

properties.name: payload
properties.description: Payload to be sent

properties.name: type
properties.description: The type of event to send

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options to be used during the send process

examples.title: Send a message via websocket
Send a message via REST
examples.code:
supabase
  .channel('room1')
  .httpSend('cursor-pos', { x: Math.random(), y: Math.random() })

definitions.name: subscribe
definitions.description: Subscribe registers your client with the server

properties.name: broadcast
properties.description:
self option enables client to receive message it broadcast
ack option instructs server to acknowledge that broadcast message was received
replay option instructs server to replay broadcast messages

properties.name: presence
properties.description: key option is used to track presence payload across clients

properties.name: private
properties.description: defines if the channel is private or not and if RLS policies will be used to check data

properties.name: topic
properties.description: Topic name can be any string.

definitions.name: teardown
definitions.description: Destroys and stops related timers.

definitions.name: track
definitions.description:
Sends the supplied payload to the presence tracker so other subscribers can see that this
client is online. Use `untrack` to stop broadcasting presence for the same key.

definitions.name: unsubscribe
definitions.description:
Leaves the channel.

Unsubscribes from server events, and instructs channel to terminate on server.
Triggers onClose() hooks.

To receive leave acknowledgements, use the a `receive` hook to bind to the server ack, ie:
channel.unsubscribe().receive("ok", () => alert("left!") )

definitions.name: untrack
definitions.description: Removes the current presence state for this client.

definitions.name: updateJoinPayload
definitions.description:
Updates the payload that will be sent the next time the channel joins (reconnects).
Useful for rotating access tokens or updating config without re-creating the channel.

Topics are automatically prefixed with `realtime:` to match the Realtime service.
If a channel with the same topic already exists it will be returned instead of creating
a duplicate connection.

For customized logging, `this.logger` can be overridden in Client constructor.

definitions.name: onHeartbeat
definitions.description:
Sets a callback that receives lifecycle events for internal heartbeat messages.
Useful for instrumenting connection health (e.g. sent/ok/timeout/disconnected).

definitions.name: push
definitions.description:
Push out a message if the socket is connected.

If the socket is not connected, the message gets enqueued within a local buffer, and sent out when a connection is next established.

definitions.name: setAuth
definitions.description:
Sets the JWT access token used for channel subscription authorization and Realtime RLS.

If param is null it will use the `accessToken` callback function or the token set on the client.

On callback used, it will set the value of the token internal to the client.

When a token is explicitly provided, it will be preserved across channel operations
(including removeChannel and resubscribe). The `accessToken` callback will not be
invoked until `setAuth()` is called without arguments.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: A JWT string to override the token set on the client.

definitions.name: createBucket
definitions.description: Creates a new Storage bucket
remarks:
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: `insert`
  - `objects` table permissions: none
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A unique identifier for the bucket you are creating.

properties.name: allowedMimeTypes
properties.description:
specifies the allowed mime types that this bucket can accept during upload.
The default value is null, which allows files with all mime types to be uploaded.
Each mime type specified can be a wildcard, e.g. image/*, or a specific mime type, e.g. image/png.

properties.name: fileSizeLimit
properties.description:
specifies the max file size in bytes that can be uploaded to this bucket.
The global file size limit takes precedence over this value.
The default value is null, which doesn't set a per bucket file size limit.

properties.name: public
properties.description: The visibility of the bucket. Public buckets don't require an authorization token to download objects, but still require a valid token for all other operations. By default, buckets are private.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
(private-beta) specifies the bucket type. see `BucketType` for more details.
  - default bucket type is `STANDARD`

examples.title: Create bucket

definitions.name: deleteBucket
definitions.description:
Deletes an existing bucket. A bucket can't be deleted with existing objects inside it.
You must first `empty()` the bucket.
remarks:
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: `select` and `delete`
  - `objects` table permissions: none
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the bucket you would like to delete.

examples.title: Delete bucket
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .deleteBucket('avatars')

definitions.name: emptyBucket
definitions.description: Removes all objects inside a single bucket.
remarks:
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: `select`
  - `objects` table permissions: `select` and `delete`
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the bucket you would like to empty.

examples.title: Empty bucket
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .emptyBucket('avatars')

definitions.name: copy
definitions.description: Copies an existing file to a new path in the same bucket.
remarks:
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: none
  - `objects` table permissions: `insert` and `select`
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works

parameters.name: fromPath
parameters.description: The original file path, including the current file name. For example `folder/image.png`.

parameters.name: toPath
parameters.description: The new file path, including the new file name. For example `folder/image-copy.png`.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: The destination options.

examples.title: Copy file
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .copy('public/avatar1.png', 'private/avatar2.png')

definitions.name: createSignedUploadUrl
definitions.description:
Creates a signed upload URL.
Signed upload URLs can be used to upload files to the bucket without further authentication.
They are valid for 2 hours.
remarks:
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: none
  - `objects` table permissions: `insert`
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path, including the current file name. For example `folder/image.png`.

properties.name: upsert
properties.description: If set to true, allows the file to be overwritten if it already exists.

examples.title: Create Signed Upload URL
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .createSignedUploadUrl('folder/cat.jpg')

definitions.name: createSignedUrl
definitions.description: Creates a signed URL. Use a signed URL to share a file for a fixed amount of time.
remarks:
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: none
  - `objects` table permissions: `select`
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path, including the current file name. For example `folder/image.png`.

parameters.name: expiresIn
parameters.description: The number of seconds until the signed URL expires. For example, `60` for a URL which is valid for one minute.

properties.name: download
properties.description: triggers the file as a download if set to true. Set this parameter as the name of the file if you want to trigger the download with a different filename.

properties.name: transform
properties.description: Transform the asset before serving it to the client.

properties.name: format
properties.description:
Specify the format of the image requested.

When using 'origin' we force the format to be the same as the original image.
When this option is not passed in, images are optimized to modern image formats like Webp.

properties.name: height
properties.description: The height of the image in pixels.

properties.name: quality
properties.description:
Set the quality of the returned image.
A number from 20 to 100, with 100 being the highest quality.
Defaults to 80

properties.name: resize
properties.description:
The resize mode can be cover, contain or fill. Defaults to cover.
Cover resizes the image to maintain it's aspect ratio while filling the entire width and height.
Contain resizes the image to maintain it's aspect ratio while fitting the entire image within the width and height.
Fill resizes the image to fill the entire width and height. If the object's aspect ratio does not match the width and height, the image will be stretched to fit.

properties.name: width
properties.description: The width of the image in pixels.

examples.title: Create Signed URL
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .createSignedUrl('folder/avatar1.png', 60)

examples.title: Create a signed URL for an asset with transformations
Create a signed URL which triggers the download of the asset

definitions.name: createSignedUrls
definitions.description: Creates multiple signed URLs. Use a signed URL to share a file for a fixed amount of time.
remarks:
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: none
  - `objects` table permissions: `select`
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works

parameters.name: paths
parameters.description: The file paths to be downloaded, including the current file names. For example `['folder/image.png', 'folder2/image2.png']`.

parameters.name: expiresIn
parameters.description: The number of seconds until the signed URLs expire. For example, `60` for URLs which are valid for one minute.

properties.name: download
properties.description: triggers the file as a download if set to true. Set this parameter as the name of the file if you want to trigger the download with a different filename.

examples.title: Create Signed URLs
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .createSignedUrls(['folder/avatar1.png', 'folder/avatar2.png'], 60)

definitions.name: download
definitions.description: Downloads a file from a private bucket. For public buckets, make a request to the URL returned from `getPublicUrl` instead.
remarks:
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: none
  - `objects` table permissions: `select`
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The full path and file name of the file to be downloaded. For example `folder/image.png`.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: Additional fetch parameters like signal for cancellation. Supports standard fetch options including cache control.

properties.name: cache
properties.description:
Controls how the request interacts with the browser's HTTP cache.
- 'default': Use standard cache behavior
- 'no-store': Bypass cache entirely (useful in Edge Functions)
- 'reload': Bypass cache but update it with response
- 'no-cache': Validate with server before using cached response
- 'force-cache': Use cache even if stale
- 'only-if-cached': Only use cache, fail if not cached

properties.name: signal
properties.description: Pass in an AbortController's signal to cancel the request.

examples.title: Download file
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .download('folder/avatar1.png')

examples.title: Download file with transformations
Download with cache control (useful in Edge Functions)
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .download('folder/avatar1.png', {}, { cache: 'no-store' })

examples.title: Download with abort signal
examples.code:
const controller = new AbortController()
setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 5000)

const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .download('folder/avatar1.png', {}, { signal: controller.signal })

definitions.name: exists
definitions.description: Checks the existence of a file.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path, including the file name. For example `folder/image.png`.

examples.title: Check file existence
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .exists('folder/avatar1.png')

definitions.name: getPublicUrl
definitions.description:
A simple convenience function to get the URL for an asset in a public bucket. If you do not want to use this function, you can construct the public URL by concatenating the bucket URL with the path to the asset.
This function does not verify if the bucket is public. If a public URL is created for a bucket which is not public, you will not be able to download the asset.
remarks:
- The bucket needs to be set to public, either via [updateBucket()](/docs/reference/javascript/storage-updatebucket) or by going to Storage on [supabase.com/dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard), clicking the overflow menu on a bucket and choosing "Make public"
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: none
  - `objects` table permissions: none
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The path and name of the file to generate the public URL for. For example `folder/image.png`.

properties.name: download
properties.description: Triggers the file as a download if set to true. Set this parameter as the name of the file if you want to trigger the download with a different filename.

properties.name: transform
properties.description: Transform the asset before serving it to the client.

properties.name: format
properties.description:
Specify the format of the image requested.

When using 'origin' we force the format to be the same as the original image.
When this option is not passed in, images are optimized to modern image formats like Webp.

properties.name: height
properties.description: The height of the image in pixels.

properties.name: quality
properties.description:
Set the quality of the returned image.
A number from 20 to 100, with 100 being the highest quality.
Defaults to 80

properties.name: resize
properties.description:
The resize mode can be cover, contain or fill. Defaults to cover.
Cover resizes the image to maintain it's aspect ratio while filling the entire width and height.
Contain resizes the image to maintain it's aspect ratio while fitting the entire image within the width and height.
Fill resizes the image to fill the entire width and height. If the object's aspect ratio does not match the width and height, the image will be stretched to fit.

properties.name: width
properties.description: The width of the image in pixels.

const { data } = supabase
  .storage
  .from('public-bucket')
  .getPublicUrl('folder/avatar1.png')

Retrieves the details of an existing file.

Returns detailed file metadata including size, content type, and timestamps.
Note: The API returns `last_modified` field, not `updated_at`.

parameters.description: The file path, including the file name. For example `folder/image.png`.

if (data) {

  console.log('Size:', data.size)
}

definitions.name: list
definitions.description:
Lists all the files and folders within a path of the bucket.

**Important:** For folder entries, fields like `id`, `updated_at`, `created_at`,
`last_accessed_at`, and `metadata` will be `null`. Only files have these fields populated.
Additionally, deprecated fields like `bucket_id`, `owner`, and `buckets` are NOT returned
by this method.
remarks:
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: none
  - `objects` table permissions: `select`
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The folder path.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Search options including limit (defaults to 100), offset, sortBy, and search

properties.name: limit
properties.description: The number of files you want to be returned.

properties.name: offset
properties.description: The starting position.

properties.name: search
properties.description: The search string to filter files by.

properties.name: sortBy
properties.description: The column to sort by. Can be any column inside a FileObject.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: Optional fetch parameters including signal for cancellation

properties.name: cache
properties.description:
Controls how the request interacts with the browser's HTTP cache.
- 'default': Use standard cache behavior
- 'no-store': Bypass cache entirely (useful in Edge Functions)
- 'reload': Bypass cache but update it with response
- 'no-cache': Validate with server before using cached response
- 'force-cache': Use cache even if stale
- 'only-if-cached': Only use cache, fail if not cached

properties.name: signal
properties.description: Pass in an AbortController's signal to cancel the request.

properties.name: created_at
properties.description: Creation timestamp (null for folders)

properties.name: id
properties.description: Unique identifier for the file (null for folders)

properties.name: metadata
properties.description: File metadata including size, mimetype, etc. (null for folders)

properties.name: cacheControl
properties.description: Cache control directive (e.g., "max-age=3600")

properties.name: contentLength
properties.description: Content length in bytes (usually same as size)

properties.name: eTag
properties.description: Entity tag for caching and conditional requests

properties.name: httpStatusCode
properties.description: HTTP status code from the storage backend

properties.name: lastModified
properties.description: Last modification timestamp (ISO 8601)

properties.name: mimetype
properties.description: MIME type of the file

properties.name: size
properties.description: File size in bytes

properties.name: name
properties.description: File or folder name (relative to the prefix) - always present

properties.name: updated_at
properties.description: Last update timestamp (null for folders)

examples.title: List files in a bucket
Search files in a bucket
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .list('folder', {
    limit: 100,
    offset: 0,
    sortBy: { column: 'name', order: 'asc' },
    search: 'jon'
  })

definitions.name: listV2
definitions.description:
Lists all the files and folders within a bucket using the V2 API with pagination support.

**Important:** Folder entries in the `folders` array only contain `name` and optionally `key` —
they have no `id`, timestamps, or `metadata` fields. Full file metadata is only available
on entries in the `objects` array.

- When `false` (default), all objects are listed as flat key/value pairs.

definitions.name: update
definitions.description: Replaces an existing file at the specified path with a new one.
remarks:
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: none
  - `objects` table permissions: `update` and `select`
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works
- For React Native, using either `Blob`, `File` or `FormData` does not work as intended. Update file using `ArrayBuffer` from base64 file data instead, see example below.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The relative file path. Should be of the format `folder/subfolder/filename.png`. The bucket must already exist before attempting to update.

parameters.name: fileBody
parameters.description: The body of the file to be stored in the bucket.

parameters.name: fileOptions
parameters.description: Optional file upload options including cacheControl, contentType, upsert, and metadata.

properties.name: cacheControl
properties.description: The number of seconds the asset is cached in the browser and in the Supabase CDN. This is set in the `Cache-Control: max-age=<seconds>` header. Defaults to 3600 seconds.

properties.name: contentType
properties.description: the `Content-Type` header value. Should be specified if using a `fileBody` that is neither `Blob` nor `File` nor `FormData`, otherwise will default to `text/plain;charset=UTF-8`.

properties.name: duplex
properties.description: The duplex option is a string parameter that enables or disables duplex streaming, allowing for both reading and writing data in the same stream. It can be passed as an option to the fetch() method.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: Optionally add extra headers

properties.name: metadata
properties.description: The metadata option is an object that allows you to store additional information about the file. This information can be used to filter and search for files. The metadata object can contain any key-value pairs you want to store.

properties.name: upsert
properties.description: When upsert is set to true, the file is overwritten if it exists. When set to false, an error is thrown if the object already exists. Defaults to false.

examples.title: Update file
examples.code:
const avatarFile = event.target.files[0]
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .update('public/avatar1.png', avatarFile, {
    cacheControl: '3600',
    upsert: true
  })

examples.title: Update file using `ArrayBuffer` from base64 file data
examples.code:
import {decode} from 'base64-arraybuffer'

const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .update('public/avatar1.png', decode('base64FileData'), {
    contentType: 'image/png'
  })

definitions.name: upload
definitions.description: Uploads a file to an existing bucket.
remarks:
- RLS policy permissions required:
  - `buckets` table permissions: none
  - `objects` table permissions: only `insert` when you are uploading new files and `select`, `insert` and `update` when you are upserting files
- Refer to the [Storage guide](/docs/guides/storage/security/access-control) on how access control works
- For React Native, using either `Blob`, `File` or `FormData` does not work as intended. Upload file using `ArrayBuffer` from base64 file data instead, see example below.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path, including the file name. Should be of the format `folder/subfolder/filename.png`. The bucket must already exist before attempting to upload.

parameters.name: fileBody
parameters.description: The body of the file to be stored in the bucket.

parameters.name: fileOptions
parameters.description: Optional file upload options including cacheControl, contentType, upsert, and metadata.

properties.name: cacheControl
properties.description: The number of seconds the asset is cached in the browser and in the Supabase CDN. This is set in the `Cache-Control: max-age=<seconds>` header. Defaults to 3600 seconds.

properties.name: contentType
properties.description: the `Content-Type` header value. Should be specified if using a `fileBody` that is neither `Blob` nor `File` nor `FormData`, otherwise will default to `text/plain;charset=UTF-8`.

properties.name: duplex
properties.description: The duplex option is a string parameter that enables or disables duplex streaming, allowing for both reading and writing data in the same stream. It can be passed as an option to the fetch() method.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: Optionally add extra headers

properties.name: metadata
properties.description: The metadata option is an object that allows you to store additional information about the file. This information can be used to filter and search for files. The metadata object can contain any key-value pairs you want to store.

properties.name: upsert
properties.description: When upsert is set to true, the file is overwritten if it exists. When set to false, an error is thrown if the object already exists. Defaults to false.

examples.title: Upload file
examples.code:
const avatarFile = event.target.files[0]
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .upload('public/avatar1.png', avatarFile, {
    cacheControl: '3600',
    upsert: false
  })

examples.title: Upload file using `ArrayBuffer` from base64 file data
examples.code:
import { decode } from 'base64-arraybuffer'

const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .upload('public/avatar1.png', decode('base64FileData'), {
    contentType: 'image/png'
  })

definitions.name: uploadToSignedUrl

remarks:

**Note:** The `upsert` option has no effect here. To enable upsert behavior,
pass `{ upsert: true }` when calling `createSignedUploadUrl()` instead.

properties.name: cacheControl

properties.name: contentType
properties.description: the `Content-Type` header value. Should be specified if using a `fileBody` that is neither `Blob` nor `File` nor `FormData`, otherwise will default to `text/plain;charset=UTF-8`.

properties.name: duplex
properties.description: The duplex option is a string parameter that enables or disables duplex streaming, allowing for both reading and writing data in the same stream. It can be passed as an option to the fetch() method.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: Optionally add extra headers

properties.name: metadata
properties.description: The metadata option is an object that allows you to store additional information about the file. This information can be used to filter and search for files. The metadata object can contain any key-value pairs you want to store.

properties.name: upsert
properties.description: When upsert is set to true, the file is overwritten if it exists. When set to false, an error is thrown if the object already exists. Defaults to false.

examples.title: Upload to a signed URL
examples.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .storage
  .from('avatars')
  .uploadToSignedUrl('folder/cat.jpg', 'token-from-createSignedUploadUrl', file)

definitions.name: from
definitions.description:
Get an Iceberg REST Catalog client configured for a specific analytics bucket
Use this to perform advanced table and namespace operations within the bucket
The returned client provides full access to the Apache Iceberg REST Catalog API
with the Supabase `{ data, error }` pattern for consistent error handling on all operations.

**Public alpha:** This API is part of a public alpha release and may not be available to your account type.
remarks:
This method provides a bridge between Supabase's bucket management and the standard
Apache Iceberg REST Catalog API. The bucket name maps to the Iceberg warehouse parameter.
All authentication and configuration is handled automatically using your Supabase credentials.

**Error Handling**: Invalid bucket names throw immediately. All catalog
operations return `{ data, error }` where errors are `IcebergError` instances from iceberg-js.
Use helper methods like `error.isNotFound()` or check `error.status` for specific error handling.
Use `.throwOnError()` on the analytics client if you prefer exceptions for catalog operations.

**Cleanup Operations**: When using `dropTable`, the `purge: true` option permanently
deletes all table data. Without it, the table is marked as deleted but data remains.

**Library Dependency**: The returned catalog wraps `IcebergRestCatalog` from iceberg-js.
For complete API documentation and advanced usage, refer to the
[iceberg-js documentation](https://supabase.github.io/iceberg-js/).

parameters.name: bucketName
parameters.description: The name of the analytics bucket (warehouse) to connect to

examples.title: Get catalog and create table
List tables in namespace
Working with namespaces
Cleanup operations

definitions.name: constructor
definitions.description: Create a new client for use in the browser.

parameters.name: supabaseUrl
parameters.description: The unique Supabase URL which is supplied when you create a new project in your project dashboard.

parameters.name: supabaseKey
parameters.description: The unique Supabase Key which is supplied when you create a new project in your project dashboard.

properties.name: accessToken
properties.description:
Optional function for using a third-party authentication system with
Supabase. The function should return an access token or ID token (JWT) by
obtaining it from the third-party auth SDK. Note that this
function may be called concurrently and many times. Use memoization and
locking techniques if this is not supported by the SDKs.

When set, the `auth` namespace of the Supabase client cannot be used.
Create another client if you wish to use Supabase Auth and third-party
authentications concurrently in the same application.

properties.name: autoRefreshToken
properties.description: Automatically refreshes the token for logged-in users. Defaults to true.

properties.name: debug
properties.description: If debug messages for authentication client are emitted. Can be used to inspect the behavior of the library.

properties.name: detectSessionInUrl
properties.description:
Detect a session from the URL. Used for OAuth login callbacks. Defaults to true.

Can be set to a function to provide custom logic for determining if a URL contains
a Supabase auth callback. The function receives the current URL and parsed parameters,
and should return true if the URL should be processed as a Supabase auth callback.

This is useful when your app uses other OAuth providers (e.g., Facebook Login) that
also return access_token in the URL fragment, which would otherwise be incorrectly
intercepted by Supabase Auth.

properties.name: flowType
properties.description: OAuth flow to use - defaults to implicit flow. PKCE is recommended for mobile and server-side applications.

properties.name: lock
properties.description: Provide your own locking mechanism based on the environment. By default no locking is done at this time.

properties.name: persistSession
properties.description: Whether to persist a logged-in session to storage. Defaults to true.

properties.name: storage
properties.description: A storage provider. Used to store the logged-in session.

properties.name: storageKey
properties.description: Optional key name used for storing tokens in local storage.

properties.name: throwOnError
properties.description:
If there is an error with the query, throwOnError will reject the promise by
throwing the error instead of returning it as part of a successful response.

properties.name: userStorage
properties.description:
A storage provider to store the user profile separately from the session.
Useful when you need to store the session information in cookies,
without bloating the data with the redundant user object.

properties.name: db
properties.description: The Postgres schema which your tables belong to. Must be on the list of exposed schemas in Supabase. Defaults to `public`.

properties.name: timeout
properties.description:
Optional timeout in milliseconds for PostgREST requests.
When set, requests will automatically abort after this duration to prevent indefinite hangs.

properties.name: urlLengthLimit
properties.description:
Maximum URL length in characters before warnings/errors are triggered.
Defaults to 8000 characters. Used to provide helpful hints when URLs
exceed server limits.

properties.name: fetch
properties.description: A custom `fetch` implementation.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: Optional headers for initializing the client.

properties.name: realtime
properties.description: Options passed to the realtime-js instance

properties.name: auth
properties.description: Supabase Auth allows you to create and manage user sessions for access to data that is secured by access policies.

properties.name: broadcast
properties.description:
self option enables client to receive message it broadcast
ack option instructs server to acknowledge that broadcast message was received
replay option instructs server to replay broadcast messages

properties.name: presence
properties.description: key option is used to track presence payload across clients

properties.name: private
properties.description: defines if the channel is private or not and if RLS policies will be used to check data

properties.name: topic
properties.description: Topic name can be any string.

properties.name: storage
properties.description: Supabase Storage allows you to manage user-generated content, such as photos or videos.

properties.name: supabaseKey
properties.description: The unique Supabase Key which is supplied when you create a new project in your project dashboard.

properties.name: supabaseUrl
properties.description: The unique Supabase URL which is supplied when you create a new project in your project dashboard.

properties.name: from
properties.description: Perform a query on a table or a view.

With custom schemas
By default the API server points to the  schema. You can enable other database schemas within the Dashboard.
Go to [Settings > API > Exposed schemas](/dashboard/project/_/settings/api) and add the schema which you want to expose to the API.

Note: each client connection can only access a single schema, so the code above can access the  schema but cannot access the  schema.

and store the encryption key in Expo SecureStore.
The  library, a reputable JavaScript-only implementation of the AES encryption algorithm in CTR mode.
A new 256-bit encryption key is generated using the  library.
This key is stored inside Expo's SecureStore, while the value is encrypted and placed inside AsyncStorage.

Please make sure that:
- You keep the ,  and  libraries up-to-date.
- Choose the correct [](https://docs.expo.dev/versions/latest/sdk/securestore/#securestoreoptions) for your app's needs.

definitions.name: removeAllChannels
definitions.description: Unsubscribes and removes all Realtime channels from Realtime client.
remarks: - Removing channels is a great way to maintain the performance of your project's Realtime service as well as your database if you're listening to Postgres changes. Supabase will automatically handle cleanup 30 seconds after a client is disconnected, but unused channels may cause degradation as more clients are simultaneously subscribed.

examples.title: Remove all channels
examples.code: supabase.removeAllChannels()

definitions.name: removeChannel
definitions.description: Unsubscribes and removes Realtime channel from Realtime client.
remarks: - Removing a channel is a great way to maintain the performance of your project's Realtime service as well as your database if you're listening to Postgres changes. Supabase will automatically handle cleanup 30 seconds after a client is disconnected, but unused channels may cause degradation as more clients are simultaneously subscribed.

parameters.description: The name of the Realtime channel.

properties.description:
self option enables client to receive message it broadcast

replay option instructs server to replay broadcast messages
